text.title: Proposal Red Team Checklist Decision Tree
text.summary: Tree mapping observable signals (trigger present, owner named, killer-anchor populated, pause-point size, staleness) to run / suppress / repair-anchors / refresh-cadence.

decision-tree.root-question: scheduled trigger (weekly fixed-price bid block) fired?

conclusion.verdict: skip-this-methodology — fire only on named trigger; do not run ad-hoc
defer; do not run anonymous checklist; resolve ownership first

branch.when: trigger_fired == true AND owner_missing == false AND stale == true

conclusion.verdict: refresh-cadence: run quarterly review first; only then run session

branch.when: trigger_fired == true AND owner_missing == false AND stale == false AND anchors_missing == true

conclusion.verdict: repair-anchors: re-populate failing items with real incident_id/postmortem/policy; reject decorative items

branch.when: trigger_fired == true AND owner_missing == false AND stale == false AND anchors_missing == false AND size_ok == false

conclusion.verdict: split pause-points > 9 items; merge or extend pause-points < 5

branch.when: trigger_fired == true AND owner_missing == false AND stale == false AND anchors_missing == false AND size_ok == true

conclusion.verdict: run-the-checklist in declared mode (READ-DO or DO-CONFIRM); record findings
preconditions not met; route to correct methodology or skip.

text.example: trigger=Friday block → owner=dm-handle → last_reviewed=2026-04-10 (fresh) → all anchors present → all pause-points 5-9 → run DO-CONFIRM session.
